named_technique: Golomb a^2 v^3 parametrization + Kronecker simultaneous approximation
bridge_lemma: For every M>=1, pick distinct squarefree v_1,...,v_M>1 with alpha_i := v_i^{3/2}; by Kronecker simultaneous density on the Q-linearly-independent vector (1, alpha_1^{-1},...,alpha_M^{-1}), infinitely many n satisfy {n*alpha_i^{-1}}>1-alpha_i^{-1} for all i, giving integers u_i:=ceil(n*alpha_i^{-1}) with u_i*alpha_i in [n,n+1) hence powerful u_i^2*v_i^3 in [n^2,(n+1)^2). Distinctness from Golomb uniqueness of the squarefree-cube part.
informal_proof_outline: GOAL: limsup_{n->infinity} h(n) = top, where h(n) = #{m Powerful : n^2 <= m < (n+1)^2}. Equivalently, for every M, infinitely many n satisfy h(n) >= M. STRATEGY: Golomb a^2*v^3 parametrization + Kronecker simultaneous density. STEP 1 (Golomb forward, EASY): for every u in N and squarefree v in N, u^2*v^3 is Powerful. Proof by primeFactors case-split: p | v => v_p >= 3; p | u not v => v_p >= 2. Mathlib: Nat.Powerful, Nat.factorization, Nat.Squarefree. ~10 lines. STEP 2 (Squarefree picks): for any M, the first M primes p_1<...<p_M are distinct squarefree >1. Set v_i:=p_i, alpha_i:=p_i^{3/2}, beta_i:=alpha_i^{-1}. STEP 3 (Q-linear independence): 1, p_1^{3/2}, ..., p_M^{3/2} are Q-linearly independent. Reason: p_i^{3/2} = p_i*sqrt(p_i) lives in Q(sqrt(p_i)); the compositum Q(sqrt(p_1),...,sqrt(p_M)) has Q-dim 2^M (Besicovitch 1940; quadratic linear-disjointness for distinct primes). Then 1, p_i^{3/2} also Q-linearly independent. STEP 4 (Kronecker simultaneous density): the orbit (n*beta_1,...,n*beta_M) mod 1 is dense in [0,1)^M. STEP 5 (Box-hit): target box B = prod_i (1-beta_i, 1) has positive Lebesgue measure; by Step 4 infinitely many n have (n*beta_i mod 1) in B for all i. STEP 6 (Construct powerful M-tuple): for such n, set u_i:=ceil(n*beta_i). Then u_i in [n*beta_i, n*beta_i + beta_i) subset [n*beta_i, (n+1)*beta_i), so u_i*alpha_i in [n, n+1), hence m_i := u_i^2*v_i^3 in [n^2, (n+1)^2). The m_i are Powerful (Step 1) and distinct (Golomb uniqueness: different squarefree v gives different m). For n large, u_i >= 1. STEP 7 (Conclude): infinitely many n with h(n) >= M, so limsup h(n) >= M; taking M arbitrary gives limsup = top. MATHLIB PATH: Mathlib has single-variable Dirichlet (Real.exists_int_int_abs_mul_sub_le), AddCircle ergodicity, but NOT simultaneous Dirichlet or direct Kronecker. Aristotle must autoformalize Kronecker via iterated single-variable approximation, or use Birkhoff on AddCircle^M. Fallback: if Kronecker too heavy, axiomatize and target the conditional implication (L4+L5 axiomatized) ==> limsup = top. The Golomb infrastructure (L1, L2 below) is independently Mathlib-PR-valuable.
candidate_lemmas: L1 (Golomb forward, EASY ~10 lines): for u in N and squarefree v in N, Nat.Powerful (u^2 * v^3). primeFactors case-split on p | v vs p | u.

L2 (Golomb backward, MEDIUM ~30 lines): for Powerful m in N, exist unique u in N and squarefree v in N with m = u^2 * v^3. Use Nat.factorization decomposition a_p = 2*b_p + 3*c_p with c_p = a_p mod 2.
L3 (squarefree-prime picks, TRIVIAL): for every M, Nat.nth Nat.Prime gives M distinct squarefree integers > 1.

L4 (Q-linear independence, HARD): 1, p_1^{3/2}, ..., p_M^{3/2} are Q-linearly independent for distinct primes p_i. Route: compositum Q(sqrt(p_i)) has Q-dim 2^M (Besicovitch). Mathlib may need new infrastructure.

L5 (Kronecker simultaneous density, VERY HARD): for Q-linearly-independent (1, beta_1, ..., beta_M), {(n*beta_i mod 1) : n in N} is dense in [0,1)^M. Mathlib gap. Options: (A) iterated single-variable Dirichlet + pigeonhole; (B) ergodicity on AddCircle^M.
L6 (box-hit, EASY given L5): {n : (n*beta_i mod 1) > 1-beta_i for all i} is infinite. Follows from density on a positive-measure open box.
L7 (construction, EASY given L1-L6): for n in box-hit, u_i := ceil(n*beta_i) yields M distinct powerful u_i^2 v_i^3 in [n^2, (n+1)^2).
L8 (assemble): h(n) >= M for n in box-hit, infinite. Hence limsup h(n) >= M. Take M arbitrary => limsup = top.

L9 (fallback axiomatization): if L4+L5 too heavy, axiomatize them and target the conditional implication L1+L2+L4+L5 ==> limsup = top. The L1+L2 Golomb infrastructure is independently Mathlib-PR-valuable.
honest_disclaimer: YOLO D6 FUSION DOSSIER (May 31 2026) — FRESH TARGET, VERIFIED STATEMENTS. After 3-round Grok-4 deep-iteration with web search, the team identified E942.variants.limsup as the most-tractable graduate-tagged variant of E942/E943, with the main conjectures of both problems being structural-open and beyond elementary closure. Per CLAUDE.md decision tree, structural-open problems route to FUSION lane; E942 wins on tractability. Soc. 71 (Powerful Numbers in Short Intervals) gave h(n) >> (log n / log log n)^{1/3} infinitely often. Recent: Tsz Ho Chan, arXiv:2207.08874 (2022). ROUND 3 (proof reconstruction): elementary proof uses Golomb + Kronecker simultaneous Diophantine approximation; verified via Grok deep-research with explicit construction (squarefree v_i, alpha_i := v_i^{3/2}, density on torus). ROUND 4 (Mathlib audit, Grok+web-search Mathlib4 docs): Mathlib4 has Real.exists_int_int_abs_mul_sub_le (single-variable Dirichlet, Mathlib.NumberTheory.DiophantineApproximation.Basic) and Mathlib.Dynamics.Ergodic.AddCircle (rotation ergodicity) but does NOT have simultaneous Dirichlet, Kronecker's density theorem, or the linear-independence-of-sqrt-prime-towers result of Besicovitch packaged as a lemma. ROUND 5 (fallback feasibility): if Kronecker too heavy in Mathlib, the L1+L2 Golomb infrastructure is independently Mathlib-PR-valuable; the conditional implication (axiomatized L4+L5 ==> limsup = top) is a clean Aristotle deliverable. Austral. Math. Soc. 71 PDF; Heath-Brown 1991 Math. Proc. Cambridge Philos. Soc. 109; Filaseta-Trifonov 1994 Acta Arith. 67; Chan 2022 arXiv:2207.08874. FUSION LANE CAN DELIVER: (a) L1 Golomb forward (clean ~10 lines); (b) L2 Golomb backward (~30 lines factorization); (c) conditional L1+L2+L4+L5 ==> limsup = top (with L4, L5 axiomatized). FUSION LANE CANNOT DELIVER: (1) Kronecker L5 from scratch in Mathlib (substantial new infrastructure); (2) Besicovitch L4 in full generality; (3) the OPEN main conjecture of E942 (existence of c with quantitative bounds — not the target here). Aristotle's MCGS + lemma-based informal reasoner might autoformalize Kronecker; if so, the entire chain closes.
